destinations.evidence: due_index_bounds rejects zero num_calls, zero frequency, ttl above SCHEDULER_MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, and before-start blocks; computes max_due_index and min_due_index arithmetically in O(1); clamps by num_calls; and compute_obligations only iterates the returned inclusive range.
The block-building snapshot helper uses the same due_index_bounds function, so the bounded inclusive due window is shared with block-building behavior.
destinations.snippet:
let Some((first_due_index, last_due_index)) = ritual_scheduled_verification::due_index_bounds(block_number, call.start_block as u64, call.frequency as u64, call.ttl as u64, call.num_calls as u64) else { continue; };
for index in first_due_index..=last_due_index { ... obligations.push(...) }

equivalence_argument: This is an exact in-port mapping for the due-window invariant: the central helper implements the inclusive TTL inequality with O(1) bounds, rejects invalid/no-due cases, enforces the protocol TTL cap, and both verification-state scanning and block-building snapshot scanning consume the bounded index interval.
notes: Mapped directly in both evidence files.
rationale: The later due-index-bounds candidates supersede the exact-block-only obligation rule by restoring the inclusive TTL window, applying it across verification and block-building, bounding TTL, and requiring O(1) due-index discovery rather than scanning 0..num_calls.
statement: Scheduled obligation computation must skip calls whose ttl exceeds SCHEDULER_MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, compute in O(1) the inclusive execution-index range where start_block + index * frequency <= block_number <= start_block + index * frequency + ttl, bound that range by num_calls, and return no due indices for zero num_calls, zero frequency, before-start, or after-expiry cases.
suggested_falsification_tests: Property-test due_index_bounds against a naive scan over 0..num_calls for varied start_block, frequency, ttl, block_number, and num_calls, including exact start, ttl boundary, overlapping windows, and no-due cases.
For ttl > SCHEDULER_MAX_TTL_BLOCKS and very large num_calls, assert obligation computation returns none or skips the call without scanning each index.
summary: The port computes scheduled due-index windows with a shared O(1), TTL-bounded helper used by verification and block-building paths.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Property-test due_index_bounds against a naive scan over 0..num_calls for start/frequency/ttl/block/num_calls combinations, including zero num_calls, zero frequency, before-start, after-expiry, TTL cap, and overlapping TTL windows.
title: Due scheduled index window is bounded and exact
